task_completion.description: Create interior walls to partition the empty building shell into multiple zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: New interior walls are visible on the 2D plan, dividing the original empty shell into distinct enclosed areas.

task_completion.description: Define and name the partitioned zones according to the task description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels on the 2D plan indicating room names such as 'Security Vestibule', 'Main Showroom', 'VIP Consultation Room', 'Secure Vault', and 'Employee Break Room' (at least 4 must be present).

task_completion.description: Install doors to control access between the newly created zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Door icons or 3D models are placed on the interior walls connecting the different partitioned rooms (at least 5 doors should be visible).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Main Showroom with display cases, shelves, and lighting, and the Secure Vault with heavy-duty storage racks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Display cases, shelving units, and lighting fixtures are visible in the Showroom area; storage racks or heavy shelves are visible in the Vault area.

task_completion.description: Furnish the VIP/Security areas with desks, seating, and computers, and the Break Room with seating and an appliance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Desks, chairs, and computer monitors are placed in the VIP and Security areas; seating and at least one appliance (e.g., refrigerator, microwave) are in the Break Room.

task_completion.description: Include dimension lines documenting key security clearances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers) are drawn on the 2D plan, measuring widths or clearances (e.g., across the vestibule or vault).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the SweetHome3D graphical user interface to complete the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SweetHome3D application window with the 2D plan, 3D view, and furniture catalog being actively used, rather than terminal windows or text editors modifying files directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely placed furniture items into the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The furniture list panel in SweetHome3D populates with various items, and the 3D view updates to show the placed 3D models.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Save' icon, uses the File -> Save menu, or a save dialog box is visible in the final screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in SweetHome3D. The agent starts with a bare shell of 16 walls. To achieve full points, the agent must add at least 4 new interior walls, place at least 5 doors, define at least 4 named rooms (target is 5: Security Vestibule, Main Showroom, VIP Consultation Room, Secure Vault, Employee Break Room), place over 40 total furniture items across the various rooms (including display cases, desks, seating, computers, racks, and appliances), and add dimension lines to the 2D plan.
